sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(50) — Klamath River Basin Regulations (Trinity River main stem below Lewiston Dam)
sources.quotedText: All anadromous Waters of the Klamath River Basin and Trinity River downstream of Lewiston Dam. This subsection applies only to waters of the Klamath River Basin that are accessible to anadromous salmonids. This subsection does not apply to waters of the Klamath River Basin that are inaccessible to anadromous salmon and trout, portions of the Trinity River system upstream of Lewiston Dam, and the Shasta River and tributaries upstream of Dwinnel Dam.

sources.title: Klamath River Basin Sport Fishing Regulations 2025 (CDFW)
CDFW News — Chinook Fishing to Reopen on Klamath, Trinity and Sacramento Rivers After 3-Year Closure
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 236551, "Trinity River", Stream, Trinity/Humboldt Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":236551,"gaz_name":"Trinity River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Trinity"/"Humboldt"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13771187.9167, 5039645.9252) converts to lon -123.708686, lat 41.184854, at the river's mouth (confluence with the Klamath River at Weitchpec, CA), replacing the file's previous coordinate (-123.2, 40.85), which was off by roughly 56 km and appears to have been a guessed estimate rather than a GNIS pull. Corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox mouth coordinate for the Trinity River, 41°11′5″N 123°42′31″W (41.18472°N, 123.70861°W).

parameters.counting_rule: Late spring-run Chinook Salmon, any size. Basin-wide (Klamath-Trinity system), per CDFW's May 2026 reopening announcement (ending the 3-year closure).

regulations.humanSummary: Late spring-run Chinook Salmon (Jul. 1–Aug. 31, 2026): 1 fish/day of any size, 2 in possession — following the 2026 reopening after a 3-year closure
regulations.verbatimText: Daily bag limit: one fish of any size. Possession limit: two fish.

parameters.counting_rule: Fall-run Chinook Salmon: 2/day total (of which at most 1 may be an adult over 23 in.); 6 in possession (of which at most 3 may be adults over 23 in.). Per CDFW's May 2026 reopening announcement — verify current in-season status, as quotas can trigger further restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: Fall-run Chinook Salmon (Sep. 1–Dec. 31, 2026): 2 fish/day (max 1 adult >23 in.), 6 in possession (max 3 adult) — following the 2026 reopening; verify current status before fishing
regulations.verbatimText: Daily bag limit: two fish, only one of which can be an adult greater than 23 inches. Possession limit: six fish, of which only three may be adults greater than 23 inches.

parameters.note: Applies basin-wide to all anadromous waters of the Klamath River Basin, including the Trinity River.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used anywhere on the Trinity River
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used. (For definitions regarding legal hook types, hook gaps, and rigging see Section 2.10.)

parameters.card_name: Sport fish report card (North Coast salmon/steelhead)
parameters.note: See Section 1.74 for sport fish report card requirements.

regulations.humanSummary: A sport fish report card is required per 14 CCR §1.74 when fishing for salmon or steelhead
regulations.verbatimText: See Section 1.74 for sport fish report card requirements.

parameters.counting_rule: Aggregate possession limit for hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead on the Trinity River, in addition to per-segment daily bag limits.

regulations.humanSummary: Hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead possession limit of 4 on the Trinity River
regulations.verbatimText: Trinity River: 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing outside Apr. 1 – Sep. 15.

regulations.humanSummary: Open Apr. 1 through Sep. 15 from 250 feet below Lewiston Dam to the Old Lewiston Bridge
regulations.verbatimText: Apr. 1 through Sep. 15. Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used in this reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used from 250 feet below Lewiston Dam to the Old Lewiston Bridge
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag limit from 250 feet below Lewiston Dam to the Old Lewiston Bridge
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **.

parameters.note: Open year-round for Brown Trout and hatchery trout/steelhead; Chinook Salmon closed under the fall-run quota (see closure rule).

regulations.humanSummary: Open Jan. 1–Jun. 30 and Jul. 1–Aug. 31 from the Old Lewiston Bridge to the Highway 299 West bridge at Cedar Flat; open year-round for trout, Chinook closed Sep. 1–Dec. 31
regulations.verbatimText: Jan. 1 through Jun. 30. ... Jul. 1 through Aug. 31. ... Sep. 1 through Dec. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: 10 Brown Trout per day, counted separately from the hatchery trout/steelhead limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 10 Brown Trout daily bag limit from the Old Lewiston Bridge to the Highway 299 West bridge at Cedar Flat
regulations.verbatimText: 10 Brown Trout.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined.

regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **.
